copyrightBoundary: Use short excerpts and project-specific analysis; do not archive or rewrite source articles.

sources.title: A shared playbook for trustworthy third party evaluations
sources.excerpt: The post frames frontier evaluation around claim type, harness choices, budgets, and visible validity checks.
sources.notes: OpenAI primary source, published 2026-05-29. Used as methodology source, not copied as article text.

sources.title: Harness Hub README
sources.excerpt: Harness Hub is documented as a personal repo harness toolkit with minimal lifecycle, standard skills, validation, and insight publishing.
sources.notes: Repo-local role and lifecycle source at current fetched main commit.

sources.title: Insight Publishing
sources.excerpt: Insight publishing turns source material into a source-backed project insight post and GitHub Pages output.
sources.notes: Defines post.json, source-ledger.json, effective-interact input, validation, and publish boundary.

sources.title: Agent Readiness Analysis
sources.excerpt: Agent readiness is a read-only analysis of context, outcomes, verification, routing, automation candidates, and learning capture.
sources.notes: Shows the existing read-only assessment surface that can absorb evaluation-readiness ideas.

sources.title: Capability Map
sources.excerpt: Harness Hub exposes a skill set, one minimal bootstrap harness, lifecycle CLI, and explicit insight publishing capability.
sources.notes: Used to keep integration proposals within the existing minimal-only architecture.

sourceClaims.statement: OpenAI frames trustworthy third-party evaluation as evidence for claims about frontier model capabilities and safeguards, not as a standalone benchmark score.
OpenAI separates evaluation claims into capability elicitation, safeguard performance, and comparison, and recommends reporting what claim the setup is meant to support.
OpenAI uses harness to mean the model-facing setup around a task, including prompts, tools, interfaces, control logic, memory, retries, validators, and related structures.
OpenAI argues that reports should expose budget details such as turns, tokens, retries, time, cost, and expected cost per successful solve where applicable.
OpenAI lists reward hacking, refusals, contamination, broken problems, and sandbagging as validity hazards that can distort evaluation results.
For OpenAI capability evaluations, the article says evaluators are asked to use Codex as a common floor so tests run through an agentic interface users are likely to rely on.
Harness Hub is a personal repo harness toolkit for making agent work repeatable across projects through analysis, skill installation, harness initialization, validation, and lifecycle commands.
Harness Hub's validate-harness surface already checks required files, QA boundaries, agent architecture boundaries, trigger hygiene, five subsystems, verification detection, and benchmark summary.
Harness Hub insight publishing keeps post.json, source-ledger.json, effective-interact input, and generated Pages HTML together with source attribution and fact/inference validation.
Harness Hub agent-readiness analysis is read-only and reports context budget, outcomes, verification, routing, automation candidates, and learning capture instead of writing automation.
Harness Hub's capability map keeps one minimal harness level and says new source ideas should improve that path rather than create new install tiers.
For this repo, the practical integration is a reporting rubric for claim class, harness setup, resource budget, and validity checks, not a direct package dependency.
Harness Hub already has enough primitives to iterate toward evaluation-ready reporting: source-backed insight posts, harness validation, agent readiness, and Effective Interact reports.

viewpoints.statement: 这个 OpenAI 项目的核心作用是给第三方评估报告定结构：先说明主张，再说明 harness、预算和有效性检查，最后让读者判断结论能泛化到哪里。
它与 Harness Hub 的方向高度吻合，因为两边都把模型表现看成模型加运行环境的结果，而不是模型名本身的结果。
可集成点应该是报告 schema 和验证门，而不是新增一个可安装 skill 套餐：给 validate-harness、agent-readiness 或 insight post 增加 claim、harness、budget、validity hazard 维度。
本次博客本身就是一次 repo 迭代：它用 insight-* 把外部来源、项目判断和后续行动边界保存到 GitHub Pages，而不是只在会话里做口头总结。
如果要真的评测 agent 或模型，还缺任务集、评分器、预算、轨迹日志、人审策略和污染检查；当前最稳妥的下一步是文档化评估准备度，而不是声称已经能做第三方安全评估。
